title: Present Value: In a scenario where a firm expects to sell a...
summary: Calculate the present value of a future cash flow by discounting at the appropriate rate.
prompt: In a scenario where a firm expects to sell a project in five years for a cash inflow of $100,000, what is the present value of this cash flow if the discount rate is 10%?
solution: To find the present value (PV), we use the formula: PV = Cash Flow / (1 + r)^t. Here, PV = $100,000 / (1 + 0.10)^5 = $90,909.09. This represents the value of future cash flows discounted back to today’s dollars.
